items.title: Zero units for 97110
items.detail: Therapeutic Exercise has 3s recorded but 0 billable units under the 8-minute rule.

items.title: Establish Right Shoulder Pain Baseline
items.detail:
Document the patient's current pain level (0-10 scale) and character (sharp/dull/aching) for the right shoulder, along with any functional limitations affecting ADLs like reaching or overhead activities.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Capture ROM and Strength Before Exercise
items.detail:
Record active and/or passive range of motion (in degrees) and any relevant manual muscle test grades for the right shoulder before beginning therapeutic exercise.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Document Exercise Specifics and Patient Response
items.detail:
Note the exact exercises performed, number of sets/reps, resistance level, and how the patient tolerated the activity during this session.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

latest_analysis.summary: Conversation segment reviewed: therapeutic exercise for shoulder pain
possible_clinical_impressions: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation
possible_diagnoses: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation

cpt_suggestions.label: therapeutic_exercise
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Application of therapeutic procedures to one or more areas, each 15 minutes, utilizing structured exercises designed to improve, manage, or restore physiological range of motion, physical strength, muscle endurance, and soft tissue flexibility.
documentation_requirements: Objective baseline metrics for strength (e.g., manual muscle testing grades) and range of motion measured via goniometry.
Clear descriptions of specific exercises performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance levels, and anatomical areas targeted.
Explicit documentation of skilled clinical intervention, such as verbal cues for form correction, manual assistance, or physiological monitoring.
Direct correlation between the selected exercises and the functional progression goals outlined in the patient care plan.

billing_caveats.time_system: CMS 8-Minute Rule compliant. Total cumulative timed minutes across all direct treatments determine total billable units per session.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Requires direct, one-on-one visual and manual contact by a licensed clinician or certified assistant.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Some non-Medicare lines follow the Substantial Portion Method (15-minute midpoint rules) rather than cumulative rounding.

ncci_conflict_risks: Bundled with 97140 (Manual Therapy) when executed on identical anatomical zones unless distinct time intervals and separate goals are validated.
Subject to overutilization audits if billed consistently as the sole intervention across multi-week cycles without functional progression.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when a patient presents with demonstrable deficits in joint mobility, active or passive range of motion, muscular weakness, or diminished structural endurance resulting from injury, surgery, or chronic degenerative disease processes.

soap_update.assessment: Possible clinical impressions to review: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation.
soap_update.plan: Review therapy plan, skilled minutes, and documentation support for detected treatment themes. Documentation gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.; Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise..

billing_hints: Documented 'therapeutic exercise' may support Therapeutic Exercise (97110).
latest_analysis.disclaimer: Rules-based detection requires clinician review before billing.

subjective.chief_complaint: Patient presents with right shoulder pain.
subjective.pain_scale: Not documented in session. Clinician should establish baseline pain level (0-10 scale) and character (sharp/dull/aching) at next visit.
subjective.duration: Not documented in session. Onset, frequency, and aggravating/relieving factors not captured.

objective.observation_notes: Therapeutic exercise performed for right shoulder pain. Specific exercises, sets/reps, resistance level, and patient tolerance during activity not documented in transcript. Clinician should capture detailed exercise parameters and patient response at next visit.
objective.range_of_motion: Not documented in session. Baseline active and/or passive range of motion measurements for right shoulder not recorded before therapeutic exercise.

assessment.diagnosis_summary: Patient engaged in therapeutic exercise for right shoulder pain management. Insufficient clinical documentation to establish primary diagnosis, functional baseline, or response to treatment. Baseline pain level, range of motion, manual muscle testing, and specific exercise parameters are required to establish medical necessity and track functional progress. Recommend comprehensive re-evaluation with complete baseline measurements before next skilled intervention. Documentation gaps to address: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise..

plan.follow_up_plan: Continue skilled physical therapy with therapeutic exercise for right shoulder pain. At next visit: (1) Establish baseline pain level (0-10 scale) and character; (2) Document active/passive range of motion in degrees; (3) Perform baseline manual muscle testing for involved shoulder musculature; (4) Document specific exercises prescribed, sets/reps, resistance level, and patient tolerance; (5) Establish functional goals related to ADLs (reaching, overhead activities); (6) Provide home exercise program with written instructions. Patient education regarding activity modification and exercise compliance to be reinforced. Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

compliance_gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.
Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

patient_summary.summary:
Hi Jane! Today we worked on therapeutic exercises to help with your right shoulder pain. We need to gather more detailed information about your pain level and how your shoulder moves so we can track your progress better. At your next visit, we'll establish a clear baseline and document exactly which exercises you're doing and how many repetitions. Keep up with any exercises we've discussed, and let us know if your symptoms change. You're taking an important step toward recovery!

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
